items.headline: US Futures Fall Amid Tech Sell-off and Geopolitical Uncertainty
items.summary: U.S. stock futures opened lower today as investors remained cautious due to ongoing Middle East tensions and a continued tech sell-off. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq were pressured, with concerns over elevated oil prices and bond yields reviving inflation fears. Despite this, strong buying in information technology stocks helped to limit overall losses in some markets.

items.statContext: Down 182 points, pressured by tech sector weakness.
Down 85 points in a mixed session.

items.headline: Fox Corporation Rises on Strong Pre-Market Activity
items.summary: Fox Corporation saw significant gains in pre-market trading today.

items.headline: Marathon Petroleum Surges in Pre-Market
items.summary: Shares of Marathon Petroleum were surging in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Bitcoin Depot Experiences Significant Decline
items.summary: Bitcoin Depot Inc. recorded one of the largest percentage losses today.

items.headline: PulteGroup Plunges in Early Trading
items.summary: Homebuilder PulteGroup saw a sharp decline in pre-market trading.

items.headline: Home Depot Shares Decline Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Home Depot's stock fell in early trade despite reporting quarterly earnings that exceeded analyst expectations, as sales growth at comparable stores missed some estimates.

items.headline: Akamai Technologies Drops Following Convertible Note Announcement
items.summary: Akamai Technologies experienced a decline in early trade after announcing plans to raise $2.6 billion through a convertible note offering.

items.headline: Fed Governor Waller Participates in Policy Panel; New Fed Chair Confirmed
items.summary: Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller is participating in a policy panel today. Meanwhile, Kevin Warsh has been confirmed as the new Federal Reserve Chair, concluding Jerome Powell's eight-year term. Market expectations for a possible rate hike by the US Federal Reserve in 2026 have risen to approximately 28% for the December meeting.

items.headline: Home Depot Reports Q1 Fiscal 2026 Results, Reaffirms Guidance
items.summary: The Home Depot announced first-quarter fiscal 2026 sales of $41.8 billion, an increase of 4.8% from the prior year. Net earnings were $3.3 billion, or $3.30 per diluted share, compared with $3.4 billion, or $3.45 per diluted share, in the same period last year. The company reaffirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance.

items.headline: Tech Sector Experiences Wide Performance Dispersion
items.summary: The technology sector is exhibiting a significant split in performance, continuing a trend observed since 2021. While hardware stocks tied to data center storage and semiconductors have seen surges, software stocks are lagging due to concerns that AI could disrupt their business models.

items.headline: Oil Prices Ease as Geopolitical Tensions Cool Slightly
items.summary: Oil prices were falling today after President Donald Trump announced a pause in fresh military strikes on Iran, indicating ongoing discussions towards a peace deal. Brent crude traded 1.80% lower at $110 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude fell 1.38% to $102.90 per barrel.

items.headline: Consumer Uncertainty and Housing Market Pressures Persist
items.summary: American consumers remain cautious amid rising costs and other economic concerns. Sales of previously occupied U.S. homes were essentially flat in April, reflecting a continued slump in the housing market due to elevated mortgage rates and housing affordability pressures.
